# emgine — Claude Configuration

## Project Architecture
<!-- Generated by /init — keep updated -->
- **Type**: CLI tool / JSX-to-image graphics engine + creative production framework
- **Stack**: TypeScript, React 18, Satori (JSX→SVG), Sharp (rasterization), PDFKit/jsPDF, tsx (no build step)
- **Entry point**: `cli/index.ts`
- **Key directories**:
  - `src/` — approved, render-ready compositions only (Root.tsx registry + individual composition files)
  - `cli/` — CLI commands and utils
  - `graphics/` — batch job JSON files (one per composition)
  - `prompts/` — blank reusable templates only — never filled here
  - `public/fonts/` — shared fonts (flat, TTF/OTF/WOFF only)
  - `public/images/<project-slug>/` — assets organized by project
  - `active/<project-slug>/` — per-project workbench: brief, drafts, working assets
  - `out/` — render output (gitignored)

## Dev Commands
```bash
npm run render   # Render a composition to out/
npm run preview  # Preview render (adds DRAFT watermark)
npm run batch    # Batch render all graphics/*.json jobs
npm run list     # List all registered compositions
npm run new      # Scaffold a new composition (interactive)
```

## Key Dependencies
- `satori`: Converts JSX/React components to SVG
- `sharp`: Rasterizes SVG to PNG/JPG/WebP/TIFF and handles format conversion
- `pdfkit` / `jspdf`: PDF output (raster and vector)
- `tsx`: Runs TypeScript directly — no compile step needed
- `zod`: Schema validation (CLI args / config)
- `react` / `react-dom`: JSX rendering surface for Satori

---

## Creative Framework Workflow

This project is a creative production system. Each output type has a prompt template in `prompts/`.
Current templates:
- `prompts/emgine.md` — Universal project brief: visual compositions, asset compilation, and copy fields (output-type agnostic)
- `prompts/ideation.md` — Creative ideation via parallel sub-agents. Run after brief approval, before design.md. Optional — use for client-facing or high-stakes projects only.

More templates to add as the framework grows (Ad Creatives, IG Carousel, Graphic Materials, etc.).

---

### Full workflow — start to finish

**Step 1 — Fill the prompt template**

Give Claude a brief and ask it to fill the brief:
```
Read prompts/emgine.md and fill it for:
  Project: <project name>
  Output type: <e.g. Thumbnail + UI Mockup>
  Platform: <e.g. n8n, Airtable, GHL>
  Work: <what you built or delivered>
  Result: <measurable outcome>
  Assets: public/images/<project-slug>/<filename.png> (<description>)
  Phases: visual composition, asset compilation
```

Claude creates `active/<project-slug>/` first, then writes the filled brief to `active/<project-slug>/brief.md`.
All drafts, working assets, and in-progress files for this project stay in that folder.
Review it. Adjust any variables. Approve.

**Step 2 — Creative ideation (optional — client-facing or high-stakes projects only)**

Skip this step for quick personal work. Use it when the layout direction matters — client delivery, carousel, or when you want to explore multiple directions before committing.

```
Read prompts/ideation.md and run ideation for active/<project-slug>/brief.md
```

Claude assesses complexity, recommends agent count, and waits for your approval before spawning.
After agents finish, Claude presents all directions — you pick the winner.
Output saved to `active/<project-slug>/ideation.md`.

**Step 3 — Asset checklist**

Claude reviews the brief and produces an asset checklist — different every project:
- Lists every asset needed (backgrounds, screenshots, logos, etc.)
- Flags which ones you need to provide vs. which Claude can generate or substitute
- For AI-generated assets (backgrounds, textures), writes the image prompt to `active/<project-slug>/assets.md`
- Waits for your confirmation before proceeding

Claude also outputs the ready-to-run scaffold commands based on the output types in the brief — no memorizing sizes needed:
```bash
npm run new <ProjectName>Thumbnail 432 324
npm run new <ProjectName>UI 1200 2950
```

**Step 4 — Scaffold the composition**

Run the commands Claude gave you in Step 3.

**Step 5 — Run the work session**

Paste the filled SYSTEM PROMPT block from your approved `active/<project-slug>/brief.md` as the first message.
Claude reads `docs/RENDER.md` (Satori CSS constraints) and `active/<project-slug>/design.md` (project design spec), then writes the TSX.

**Step 6 — Render and review**

```bash
npm run preview <ProjectName>    # DRAFT watermark — quick visual check
npm run render <ProjectName>     # Final PNG to out/
```

**Step 7 — Ship the output**

PNGs live in `out/`. Upload to your portfolio, client delivery, or any platform.

---

### Critical rules for composition authors (Claude or human)

- **Read `docs/RENDER.md` + `active/<project-slug>/design.md` before writing any TSX.** RENDER.md has the Satori CSS allowlist. `design.md` has the per-project design spec — palette, spacing rhythm, feel, anti-patterns. Both must be read or the output will drift.
- **Images must be base64 data URIs.** Satori cannot read file paths or fetch URLs.
- **Every root element must have `display: "flex"`.** Satori uses Yoga (React Native) layout.
- **Fonts must exist in `public/fonts/` as TTF, OTF, or WOFF.** WOFF2 is not supported and fails silently.
- **Use brand tokens from `brand.config.ts`** — never hardcode colors or spacing inline.

---

### Prompt templates — how to add a new one

1. Copy `prompts/emgine.md` to `prompts/your-output-type.md`
2. Replace the phases with the ones relevant to your output type
3. Update the VARIABLES REFERENCE table
4. Update this CLAUDE.md to list the new template under Creative Framework Workflow

---

## User Preferences
- When renaming a prompt/brief file, also update all references to the old filename in CLAUDE.md as part of the same task
- When a brief produces output files, Claude writes two files to `active/<project-slug>/` — `brief.md` and `design.md` — both reviewed together before the work session starts
- `docs/DESIGN.md` is a global reference/template only — never overwritten per project
- Per-project design specs live at `active/<project-slug>/design.md`
- When scaffolding a new project, create `active/<project-slug>/` first before writing any files — all project work (brief, design spec, asset prompts, drafts) stays in that folder
- After brief approval, always output ready-to-run scaffold commands based on output types in the brief — never make the user look up sizes
- Step numbering in workflows must be whole numbers only — no 1.5, 2.5, etc. Restructure steps instead

## Lab Notes — Do Not Try Again
- 2026-04: Asking too many clarifying questions at once causes confusion — narrow to the 2 that actually block the build, decide the rest when building
- 2026-04: PAT exposed in git remote URL — always use `git remote set-url` to update credentials, never paste tokens in plain text commands shown to user
